denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

We are denying coverage for the MRI of the wrist performed on 06/02/2026 by an out-of-network radiology center.

Your plan requires prior authorization for all advanced imaging, and specifically requires out-of-network services to receive a Network Exception authorization before the service is rendered. Our records indicate no such authorization was requested or approved. Because this was not a life-threatening emergency, you are responsible for the out-of-network charges.
clinical_context: Patient fell skateboarding and went to an urgent care. X-rays were negative but the doctor suspected a scaphoid fracture (which can cause bone death if missed). The doctor sent him immediately to the imaging center next door. The center was out-of-network and did not run a prior auth because of the urgent nature of the referral.
